subject: Re: Fwd: Barging Activities

i'll pay for survey and trip/ then we'll discuss

> I should like you to give this e mail to Mr. Epstein

> delivery of multiple containers or concrete mixers
> to the island without
> requiring modifications to the existing dock and
> without much of a hassle,
> if any, with permits. The list price is $375,000.
> 
>  
> 
> I have the specifications for the vessel and I am
> awaiting pictures. It is
> located in Baltimore. If the pictures check out it
> should be looked at and
> surveyed if there is an interest in trying to bring
> it here. I have some
> suggestions as to how this might take place: 
> 
>  
> 
> 1.  The capital, maintenance and operational cost of
> this barge would be
> high, and perhaps prohibitive, if the vessel is to
> be brought here and then
> used sporadically only for LSJ over the next 2 ½

> 2.  There are two local operators who would be
> capable of managing the
> use, operation, and maintenance of this vessel:
> Steve Rampino and Catcor
> Services which presently does the barging and towing
> for Domino’s gas
> stations here; Catcor brought me this lead and
> should be favored in putting
> something together to implement such an arrangement;
> both have active towing
> and barging businesses in St. Thomas. 
> 3.  The vessel could be purchased and dedicated to
> LSJ on a priority
> basis, but also used for other purposes to defray
> the costs; the principal
> other uses would be trips to Enighed Pond or,
> perhaps in the future, Coral
> Bay on St. John;
> 4.  As part of this project and as a project cost we
> could purchase this
> vessel as a LSJ asset or, at least initially, add
> this vessel to the RR
> fleet coupled with an operating and maintenance
> agreement with a local
> entity which could also charter the vessel for other
> trips to create some
> revenue to defray the costs of making the vessel
> available to LSJ; 
> 5.  We would be paid our costs and a fee and markup
> to manage this
> operation; the vessel could be dedicated to such a
> program for a specific
> term and then sold once the LSJ needs were met.
> 
>

> I believe that presenting a suggestion of this type

> last discussion with Mr. Epstein. I do not want to

> Epstein about this matter.

> might have has any currency,

> and delivery to St.
> Thomas. As a part of this process we would glue
> together with you an
> acceptable set of terms between ourselves and a
> clear understanding of how
> this arrangement would be paid for. 
> 
>  
> 
> Please let me know what you think. Again, it may
> make sense for me to
> discuss this with Mr. Epstein personally.

i'll pay for survey and trip/ then we'll discuss

From: "John Amerling" jamerling@rrcaribbean.com
To: "'Emad Hanna'" ehanna@nysgmail.com
CC: "Darren Indyke" dkiesq@aol.com, "'Jack
Parker'" jparker@reed-reed.com
Subject: Barging Activities
Date: Tue, 29 Aug 2006 10:13:01 -0400

I should like you to give this e mail to Mr. Epstein
and I should like to
review it with him if possible if he is on island or
will be shortly. I have
located a barge for sale, an LCU, which appears to
be very suitable for the
delivery of multiple containers or concrete mixers
to the island without
requiring modifications to the existing dock and
without much of a hassle,
if any, with permits. The list price is $375,000.

I have the specifications for the vessel and I am
awaiting pictures. It is
located in Baltimore. If the pictures check out it
should be looked at and
surveyed if there is an interest in trying to bring
it here. I have some
suggestions as to how this might take place:

  1. The capital, maintenance and operational cost of
    this barge would be
    high, and perhaps prohibitive, if the vessel is to
    be brought here and then
    used sporadically only for LSJ over the next 2 ½
    years.
  2. There are two local operators who would be
    capable of managing the
    use, operation, and maintenance of this vessel:
    Steve Rampino and Catcor
    Services which presently does the barging and towing
    for Domino’s gas
    stations here; Catcor brought me this lead and
    should be favored in putting
    something together to implement such an arrangement;
    both have active towing
    and barging businesses in St. Thomas.
  3. The vessel could be purchased and dedicated to
    LSJ on a priority
    basis, but also used for other purposes to defray
    the costs; the principal
    other uses would be trips to Enighed Pond or,
    perhaps in the future, Coral
    Bay on St. John;
  4. As part of this project and as a project cost we
    could purchase this
    vessel as a LSJ asset or, at least initially, add
    this vessel to the RR
    fleet coupled with an operating and maintenance
    agreement with a local
    entity which could also charter the vessel for other
    trips to create some
    revenue to defray the costs of making the vessel
    available to LSJ;
  5. We would be paid our costs and a fee and markup
    to manage this
    operation; the vessel could be dedicated to such a
    program for a specific
    term and then sold once the LSJ needs were met.

I believe that presenting a suggestion of this type
is consistent with my
last discussion with Mr. Epstein. I do not want to
go forward on any basis,
however, without a clear understanding with Mr.
Epstein about this matter.

I suggest that if this idea or a better one you
might have has any currency,
we look at the cost of a survey and a trip to
Baltimore to check out the
asset and if it is acceptable, explore its purchase
and delivery to St.
Thomas. As a part of this process we would glue
together with you an
acceptable set of terms between ourselves and a
clear understanding of how
this arrangement would be paid for.

Please let me know what you think. Again, it may
make sense for me to
discuss this with Mr. Epstein personally.
